name: 3D / CGI
description: Prompt cookbook for 3D / CGI / rendered video — Pixar-stylized through to photorealistic, plus low-poly, isometric, cel-shaded, and hyperreal-with-impossible-physics. Focuses on render-engine vocabulary (PBR, SSS, caustics, GI), lighting setups, camera grammar, and material science needed to push i2v models past the generic 'make it look 3D' baseline. Reference-optional: works from a brief alone, but a brand or product still triggers the reference-required gate.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter — single keyframe per shot, prompt carries the render-engine vocabulary (PBR, SSS, HDRI, caustics, lighting Kelvin temps).
stackSummary.i2v: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro via OpenRouter for default i2v at 5s/clip; veo-3.1 (premium tier) for cinematic camera moves (orbital / dolly-zoom / spiral ascent) where motion fidelity matters. generate_audio: false — audio added separately.
stackSummary.voiceover: ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 — optional. CGI sequences often run music-only; if VO is used, lean toward calm narrator timbre, not UGC peer voice. Language follows the user's brief.
stackSummary.captions: Optional. Most 3D / CGI sequences are wordless. If used: MinimalCaptions, white-on-black, low contrast — never Hormozi-style on a hyperreal render (kills the aesthetic).
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — orchestral / cinematic / ambient swell matched to the camera arc. Single track, no split.

variationAxes: render style (Pixar-stylized, painterly, cel-shaded, low-poly, isometric, hyperreal, hyperreal-with-impossible-physics)
camera arc (orbital, push-in, crane, dolly-zoom, spiral ascent, glide-through, free-floating)
lighting key (golden-hour HDRI, studio 3-point, single dramatic side, rim-dominant, neon practical, cool moonlight, volumetric god rays, caustics)
material hero (chrome, gold, glass / crystal, skin / SSS, fur, water, marble, bioluminescent)
particle effect (dust motes, swirling smoke, coalescing swarm, sparks, splash, fire, data particles, fog)
doNotCopyLiterally: Subject, exact prompt, exact camera arc, lighting direction. The vibe is the framework — render-vocab + hook + master template — not any specific scene.
referenceNotes: Reference-optional in general — the framework is built to generate from a brief alone. Reference-required gate (AGENTS.md hard rule #3) still applies if the brief names a specific brand or product: a real product photo / logo / packaging is required before any keyframe is generated. AI-improvised branding on a real product fails the gate. For pure CGI subjects (creatures, abstract, original characters) no reference is needed.
